Here's a one-page PRD outline for dark mode:

# PRD: Dark-Mode Theme

**Author:** [you] · **Status:** Draft · **Date:** 2026-06-22

## 1. Summary
Add a user-selectable dark color theme to the web app, with a light/dark/system toggle that persists across sessions and respects the OS preference by default.

## 2. Problem & Motivation
- Users in low-light environments report eye strain on the light-only UI.
- Dark mode is a baseline expectation; its absence shows up in reviews and churn feedback.
- Competitors ship it; we look dated without it.

## 3. Goals
- Ship a dark theme covering all primary screens and components.
- Toggle: Light / Dark / System, with System as the default.
- Persist the choice per user (and per device for logged-out users).

## 4. Non-Goals
- Custom/user-defined color themes.
- Per-component or scheduled (auto-switch by time) theming.
- Theming of transactional emails and marketing pages.

## 5. Success Metrics
- ≥30% of active users select dark or system within 30 days.
- No regression in WCAG AA contrast on either theme.
- No increase in render/CLS performance metrics.

## 6. User Stories
- As a user, I can switch themes from settings and see it apply immediately.
- As a returning user, my theme choice is remembered.
- As a first-time user, the app matches my OS appearance automatically.

## 7. Requirements
- **Theming layer:** centralize colors as semantic design tokens (e.g. `--bg-surface`, `--text-primary`); no hard-coded hex in components.
- **Toggle UI:** accessible control in settings and/or header; reflects current state.
- **Persistence:** store preference (logged-in: user profile; logged-out: localStorage); apply before first paint to avoid flash-of-wrong-theme.
- **System sync:** honor `prefers-color-scheme` and react to live OS changes when set to System.
- **Coverage:** all components, charts, illustrations, focus/hover/disabled states, and third-party embeds.

## 8. Design & Accessibility
- Both themes meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 text).
- Define elevation/shadow behavior for dark surfaces (shadows read poorly on dark — use surface tints).
- Audit images/logos that assume a light background.

## 9. Rollout
- Behind a feature flag → internal dogfood → staged rollout.
- Default new users to System; preserve existing users on Light until they opt in.

## 10. Open Questions
- Single global theme or per-workspace override?
- How do we handle user-uploaded content authored for light backgrounds?
- Scope of third-party/embedded components we don't fully control.

---

# PRD: Dark-Mode Theme

**Author:** [you] · **Status:** Draft · **Date:** 2026-06-22

## 1. Summary
Add a user-selectable dark color theme to the web app, with a light/dark/system toggle that persists across sessions and respects the OS preference by default.

**Recommendation:** Build dark mode now using a semantic design-token layer, ship it behind a feature flag with **System as the default for new users**, and keep existing users on Light until they opt in. This is the lowest-risk path to a baseline-expected feature; the main cost is the one-time token migration, which pays for itself the next time we theme anything.

## 2. Problem & Motivation
- Users in low-light environments report eye strain on the light-only UI.
- Dark mode is a baseline expectation; its absence shows up in reviews and churn feedback.
- Competitors ship it; we look dated without it.

## 3. Goals
- Ship a dark theme covering all primary screens and components.
- Toggle: Light / Dark / System, with System as the default.
- Persist the choice per user (and per device for logged-out users).

## 4. Non-Goals
- Custom/user-defined color themes.
- Per-component or scheduled (auto-switch by time) theming.
- Theming of transactional emails and marketing pages.

## 5. Success Metrics
> _All target figures below are proposed assumptions (TBD) pending PM/data sign-off — not measured baselines. Veto or replace any that don't match our analytics._
- **[ASSUMPTION — TBD]** ≥30% of active users select dark or system within 30 days. (Placeholder target; no baseline yet — confirm against analytics before commit.)
- No regression in WCAG AA contrast on either theme. (Standard: WCAG 2.1 AA, 4.5:1 for normal text.)
- No increase in render/CLS performance metrics. (Baseline = current production CLS/render numbers — **[TBD: pull exact values]**.)

## 6. User Stories
- As a user, I can switch themes from settings and see it apply immediately.
- As a returning user, my theme choice is remembered.
- As a first-time user, the app matches my OS appearance automatically.

## 7. Requirements
- **Theming layer:** centralize colors as semantic design tokens (e.g. `--bg-surface`, `--text-primary`); no hard-coded hex in components.
- **Toggle UI:** accessible control in settings and/or header; reflects current state.
- **Persistence:** store preference (logged-in: user profile; logged-out: localStorage); apply before first paint to avoid flash-of-wrong-theme.
- **System sync:** honor `prefers-color-scheme` and react to live OS changes when set to System.
- **Coverage:** all components, charts, illustrations, focus/hover/disabled states, and third-party embeds.

## 8. Design & Accessibility
- Both themes meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 text; source: WCAG 2.1 success criterion 1.4.3).
- Define elevation/shadow behavior for dark surfaces (shadows read poorly on dark — use surface tints).
- Audit images/logos that assume a light background.

## 9. Rollout
- Behind a feature flag → internal dogfood → staged rollout.
- Default new users to System; preserve existing users on Light until they opt in.

## 10. Open Questions
- Single global theme or per-workspace override?
- How do we handle user-uploaded content authored for light backgrounds?
- Scope of third-party/embedded components we don't fully control.

# PRD: Dark-Mode Theme

**Author:** [Author] · **Status:** Draft · **Date:** 2026-06-22

## 1. Summary
Add a user-selectable dark color theme with a Light / Dark / System toggle that persists across sessions and defaults to the OS preference.

## 2. Problem & Motivation
- Users in low-light environments report eye strain on our light-only UI.
- Dark mode is now a baseline expectation; its absence surfaces in app-store reviews and churn feedback.
- Major competitors ship it, which makes our product read as dated in side-by-side evaluations.

## 3. Goals
- Ship a dark theme covering all primary screens, components, and interactive states.
- Provide a Light / Dark / System toggle, with System as the default.
- Persist the choice per user when logged in, and per device when logged out.

## 4. Non-Goals
- Custom or user-defined color palettes.
- Per-component theming or time-of-day auto-switching.
- Theming of transactional emails and marketing pages.

## 5. Success Metrics
- **Adoption (leading):** ≥25% of weekly active users render in dark mode — system-resolved or explicitly chosen — within 30 days of GA.
- **Default-fit signal:** of users whose OS resolves to dark, <5% manually switch to Light and stay there; a higher rate means the palette or default mapping needs work.
- **Accessibility guardrail:** zero net-new WCAG AA contrast failures (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI) on either theme.
- **Performance guardrail:** no theme flash on first paint; no regression in first-contentful-paint or CLS.
- **Support guardrail:** no sustained rise in legibility or theme-related support tickets after rollout.

## 6. User Stories
- As a user, I can switch themes from settings and see the change apply immediately, with no reload.
- As a returning user, my theme choice is remembered across sessions and devices (when logged in).
- As a first-time user, the app matches my OS appearance automatically.

## 7. Requirements
- **Theming layer:** centralize colors as semantic design tokens (e.g. `--bg-surface`, `--text-primary`); no hard-coded hex in components.
- **Toggle UI:** an accessible control (keyboard- and screen-reader-operable) in settings that reflects the current state.
- **Persistence:** store the preference in the user profile when logged in and `localStorage` when logged out; apply it before first paint to avoid a flash of the wrong theme.
- **System sync:** honor `prefers-color-scheme` and react to live OS changes while set to System.
- **Coverage:** all components, charts, illustrations, focus/hover/active/disabled states, and third-party embeds.

## 8. Design & Accessibility
- Both themes meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 text, 3:1 non-text UI).
- Define dark-surface elevation with surface tints rather than shadows, which read poorly on dark.
- Audit images, logos, and icons that assume a light background; supply dark-safe variants where needed.

## 9. Rollout
- Behind a feature flag → internal dogfood → staged percentage rollout → GA.
- New users default to System; existing users stay on Light until they opt in, to avoid a jarring forced change.

## 10. Risks & Dependencies
- **Token migration:** retrofitting existing hard-coded colors is the bulk of the work and gates everything downstream.
- **Third-party components:** embeds and SDKs we don't fully control may not theme cleanly; scope these early.
- **User-uploaded content:** images and rich text authored for light backgrounds may look wrong on dark.

## 11. Open Questions
- Single global theme, or a per-workspace override?
- How do we treat user-uploaded content authored for light backgrounds — neutral framing, forced light canvas, or leave as-is?
- What is the exact scope of third-party/embedded components we commit to theming for v1?
